name: Modulatricidae
description: Modulatricidae is a small family of African passerine birds with uncertain taxonomic history. They were formerly placed with thrushes and babblers but are now recognized as a distinct lineage.

children.name: Modulatrix
children.description: A monotypic genus of thrush-like bird found in montane forests of eastern Africa.

children.name: Modulatrix stictigula
children.description: The Dapple-throat is a brownish thrush-like bird with a heavily spotted throat and breast. It skulks in the understory of montane forests in Tanzania and Mozambique.

children.name: Arcanator
children.description: A genus of two species of shy, thrush-like birds found in African montane forests.

children.name: Arcanator orostruthus
children.description: The Spot-throat is a dark brown bird with black spots on the throat and upper breast. It is a secretive inhabitant of montane forests in Tanzania and Mozambique.

children.name: Arcanator nigriceps
children.description: The Dusky Greenbul is a dark olive-brown bird with a blackish crown and pale eyes. It inhabits montane forests in the Eastern Arc Mountains of Tanzania.
